description_long: This object consists of two large, vertically-oriented cylindrical storage tanks with conical bottoms, supported by a framework of metal legs. The tanks are connected at the top by a rectangular horizontal structure, likely for piping or material transfer, and are accompanied by a rectangular shed-like building with a pitched roof adjacent to the tanks. There are also two smaller box-like structures, one of which appears to be a control or utility box, and the other a small auxiliary building. The tanks and framework are constructed from smooth, industrial-grade metal, giving the entire assembly a utilitarian appearance. The overall design is typical of storage facilities found in urban or industrial environments, optimized for bulk material storage and transfer.
description: A pair of large, vertical cylindrical storage tanks with conical bases, metal framework, and adjacent utility buildings, typical of urban or industrial storage facilities.
description_view_0: The view shows the side of the tanks with the adjacent pitched-roof building and a utility box in the foreground.
description_view_1: This view presents a direct look at the two cylindrical tanks from the side, with the pitched-roof building in front.
description_view_2: The tanks are seen from an angle, highlighting the connection between the tanks and the adjacent structures.
description_view_3: A frontal view of the tanks, showing both the main and auxiliary buildings, as well as the connecting framework at the top.
